records.ety: [L. exasperatus, p. p. of exsasperare to roughen, exasperate; ex out (intens.) + asperare to make rough, asper rough. See Asperity.]

quotes: Like swallows which the exasperate dying year Sets spinning.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Exsasperated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Exasperating (?).]

defs: To irritate in a high degree; to provoke; to enrage; to excite or to inflame the anger of; as, to exasperate a person or his feelings.
quotes: To exsasperate them against the king of France.

defs: To make grievous, or more grievous or malignant; to aggravate; to imbitter; as, to exasperate enmity.
quotes: To exasperate the ways of death.
